channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court suggests Parliament should handle reforms in Muslim inheritance laws rather than judicial intervention
item.description: On March 10, 2026, the Supreme Court of India expressed that it would be prudent to allow Parliament to implement the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) rather than judicially annul the Shariat Application Act ...

item.title: IFP focuses on local employment in KwaMashu by addressing illegal foreign nationals
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) in eThekwini District has intensified its programme addressing illegal foreign nationals, moving its focus from the Durban CBD to townships where poverty is most severe...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Offers Condolences and Reaffirms Pakistan-Iran Cooperation
item.description: On Tuesday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif sent a formal letter to Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, congratulating him on assuming the role following the assassination of his fa...

item.title: Malawi’s digital health records cut HIV deaths and improve patient care
item.description: In Malawi, one of the countries hardest hit by HIV, a simple but effective innovation has significantly improved patient outcomes: a digital medical records system. About 7% of Malawians live with HIV...

item.title: Nigerian Army Detains Major After He Withdrew Troops to Protect Soldiers
item.description: Major Shamsudeen Sani, wey don spend years dey fight Boko Haram and ISWAP for northeastern Nigeria, don dey detained by Nigerian Army for Maiduguri for months. Dem put am for solitary confinement afte...

item.title: Australian Fintech Founder Targets US Cryptocurrency Payments Market
item.description: Raagulan Pathy, a 45-year-old Australian entrepreneur originally from Adelaide, is emerging as a leading figure in the country's fintech scene. Splitting his time between the Gold Coast and Singapore,...

item.title: Trump's Response to Iran Conflict Reveals Limits to Economic Pain Tolerance, Offering Insights for China's Taiwan Strategy
item.description: In the wake of the recent U.S.-led military campaign against Iran, which involved intense strikes degrading Iran's naval, air, and missile capabilities while disrupting shipping through the Strait of ...

item.title: Drone Attacks in Erbil Escalate Regional Tensions in Northern Iraq
item.description: Erbil, the capital of Iraq’s northern Kurdistan region, is facing escalating drone and missile attacks amid the ongoing conflict between Iran, the U.S., and Israel. Despite Iraq not officially partici...

item.title: Audio reveals inconsistencies in Rep. Rob Bresnahan’s statements about stock trades
item.description: Rep. Rob Bresnahan, a Republican from Pennsylvania, is facing scrutiny over his stock trading activities, which appear to contradict his campaign pledge to stop congressional stock trading. An intervi...

item.title: Two Teenagers Arrested for Attempted Terror Attack Near New York Mayor's Residence
item.description: Two teenagers, aged 18 and 19, have been charged with terrorism-related offenses following an incident at an anti-Muslim protest in New York. Emir Balat and Ibrahim Kayumi allegedly traveled from Penn...

item.title: Kei Ishikawa’s adaptation of Kazuo Ishiguro’s A Pale View of Hills examines postwar trauma and memory in Nagasaki
item.description: Director Kei Ishikawa’s film adaptation of Kazuo Ishiguro’s 1982 debut novel, A Pale View of Hills, delves into themes of identity, memory, and the enduring legacy of the atomic bombings in Japan. The...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Grants Extension for Madlanga Commission to Complete Inquiry
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced an extension for the Judicial Commission of Inquiry into Criminality, Political Interference, and Corruption within the criminal justice system. The commission,...

item.title: Transat posts $29.5-million quarterly loss as shareholders prepare to vote on board changes
item.description: Transat A.T. Inc. (TRZ-T) announced a net loss of $29.5 million for its latest quarter ending January 31, a significant improvement from a $122.5-million loss in the same period last year. Revenue inc...

item.title: Rijiju references Rahul Gandhi's 2018 hug in Lok Sabha debate on no-confidence motion; Priyanka Gandhi responds
item.description: On 10th March 2026, during a debate in the Lok Sabha on a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla, Union Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju brought up a 2018 incident where Congress MP ...

item.title: Teenage girls' perspectives on rising low mood and anxiety: insights from a UK study
item.description: Women and girls consistently experience higher levels of low mood and anxiety compared to men and boys, a gap that has widened in recent years according to NHS and national data. Young women aged 17–1...

item.title: Judiciary Introduces Four-Day Week and Fuel Savings Measures Amid Rising Energy Costs
item.description: A top judicial committee in Pakistan, chaired by Chief Justice Yahya Afridi, has approved a series of austerity and energy conservation measures for courts across the country. These steps include the ...

item.title: CPJ Urges Chinese Embassy to Stop Online Attacks on Filipino Journalist Regine Cabato and PCIJ
item.description: The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ), a global press freedom watchdog, has called on the Chinese Embassy in the Philippines to immediately cease its online attacks against Filipino investigative...

item.title: Tanzania Ports Authority delays new port levy to give businesses time to adjust
item.description: The Tanzania Ports Authority (TPA) has decided to temporarily suspend the Port Infrastructure Development (PID) levy until July 1, 2026. This decision comes after port users, including transporters, t...

item.title: Engin Firat's Tenure as Harambee Stars Coach: Challenges and Legacy
item.description: Engin Firat, the Turkish coach who led Harambee Stars from September 2021 until December 2024, passed away recently at Istanbul Airport due to a suspected heart attack. His three-year stint in Kenya w...

item.title: Drone Strike Sparks Fire at Abu Dhabi's Ruwais Industrial Complex
item.description: Authorities in Abu Dhabi reported a fire at a facility within the Ruwais Industrial Complex, triggered by a drone attack amid escalating regional tensions. The incident occurred during the ongoing US-...
